Creating images with R

The Dud

Ryan Stevens

@import url('https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Oswald:wght@200..700&display=swap');
p {font-weight:bolder;}
h2 {color: red;}
h2 {font-family: "Open Sans", sans-serif;}
body{font-family: "Oswald", sans-serif}

Project requirements

File Explorer showing my project folder with all my project files in it

https://github.com/steyven1/stats220/tree/main

Inspo meme

Inspiration meme, depicts the Dud in the center, which is an altered version of Millhouse from The Simpsons holding a wilted flower, with the text “You got the Dud,” below the image

The key components of the meme are the altered image of Millhouse from The Simpsons with the text “YOU GOT THE DUD.” underneath, it has become a meme online where this image of “The Dud” will pop up in a video and then the subject of the video’s face will change to a face of homer smiling. This specific image acts as a sort of “Rick-roll” or “Bait and switch” meme, where the viewer might get tricked or trolled into viewing this image and in turn getting “The Dud”

My meme

My meme based off the inspiration meme, where the image of Millhouse is still present in the center of the screen, but the text has changed to text below the image saying “The Dud has been passed on”

In my version of this meme, I have changed the text to say “The Dud has been passed on.”, the implication behind this is that the viewer who is seeing this has now acquired “The Dud”, which builds on the original intention of the meme, where it acts as some sort of “Rick-Roll” or “Bait and Switch” meme, where the intention could be to make someone else view the image so they take “The Dud” from you. I made these changes to try and modify the intention of the original meme.

My animated meme

An animated version of the previous image, all components of the previous image remain in the same position, in this gif the text, which is the same as before, starts as a small size, and increases in size, it also flashes from red to black

Creativity

I showed creativity in this project by going through the creative process and modifying an image that I like, by turning it into something I wanted it to be, examples of the creative process in this final animated meme are as follow:

  1. The text grows in size as to act as a sort of surprise, as the intention of the meme is to be to pass it on or troll your friends with it by giving them “The dud”.

  2. The changing colour of the text also helps to create this sort of suprise as the flashing colour serves to be some sort of warning or be eye-catching

Learning reflection

  • One important idea I learnt from completing this project was mainly the application of all the things we learnt into making something from start to finish, I definitely feel like my knowledge of the content has been greatly accelerated during the length of this project. So essentially, I feel like from this point onward, that I will be able to better apply the course content to practical aspects much better.

  • One aspect that was covered in this project related to data technologies that I have become more curious about exploring more was the use of vectors, I think the application of vectors in analysing data sets will prove to be very insightful and useful, so I definitely look forward to that.

Appendix

Do not change, edit, or remove the R chunk included below.

If you are working within RStudio and within your Project1 RStudio project (check the top right-hand corner says “Project1”), then the code from the meme.R script will be displayed below.

This code needs to be visible for your project to be marked appropriately, as some of the criteria are based on this code being submitted.

#Loading these two libraries first to allow their contents to be run in the code (image_read, image_resize, etc..)
library(magick)
library(tidyverse)


# Assigned the value of the image to meme.image, can now refer to "meme.image" instead of referring to the image link each time
meme.image<- image_read("https://static.wikia.nocookie.net/simpsonsfanon/images/6/6c/Dudhouse.jpeg/revision/latest/thumbnail/width/360/height/450?cb=20230405094645") 


# Resized the image, and allowed for annotation and modification
stillmeme.image<- image_resize(meme.image, geometry = "500x550",
             filter = NULL) %>%
  image_annotate(text = "The Dud has \n been passed on.",
                 color = "#000000",
                 size = 45,
                 font = "Segoe Print",
                 gravity = "south",
                 location = "+0+50",
                 weight = 700)%>%
  
  
  # Write completed and edited meme to project folder under the name "Meme_DUD.png"
  image_write("Meme_DUD.png")

# A vector is used to determine the size of the font, which I call in each frame where "size = text.size[1]"
text.size <- c(15, 25, 35, 45)

font.colour <- c("#000000", "#F54927")

# Creating the frames
# This is frame 1 of the animation
frame1 <- image_resize(meme.image, geometry = "500x550",
                                         filter = NULL) %>%
  image_annotate(text = "The Dud has \n been passed on.",
                 color = font.colour[1],
                 size = text.size[1],
                 font = "Segoe Print",
                 gravity = "south",
                 location = "+0+50",
                 weight = 700)
# This is frame 2 of the animation
frame2 <- image_resize(meme.image, geometry = "500x550",
                       filter = NULL) %>%
  image_annotate(text = "The Dud has \n been passed on.",
                 color = font.colour[2],
                 size = text.size[2],
                 font = "Segoe Print",
                 gravity = "south",
                 location = "+0+50",
                 weight = 700)
# This is frame 3 of the animation
frame3 <- image_resize(meme.image, geometry = "500x550",
                       filter = NULL) %>%
  image_annotate(text = "The Dud has \n been passed on.",
                 color = font.colour[1],
                 size = text.size[3],
                 font = "Segoe Print",
                 gravity = "south",
                 location = "+0+50",
                 weight = 700)
# This is frame 4 of the animation
frame4 <- image_resize(meme.image, geometry = "500x550",
                       filter = NULL) %>%
  image_annotate(text = "The Dud has \n been passed on.",
                 color = font.colour[2],
                 size = text.size[4],
                 font = "Segoe Print",
                 gravity = "south",
                 location = "+0+50",
                 weight = 700)
# I am putting the frames in order using a vector
frames <- c(frame1, frame2, frame3, frame4)

# I am creating an animation using the image_animate function
animation <- image_animate(frames, fps = 1)

# The animation under value "animation" is being written to the project file under the name "my_animated_meme.gif"
image_write(animation, "my_animated_meme.gif")
